items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:

items.title: Aeropostale Owner Says Prices Won't Rise for Back-to-School Shopping
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>It's the second biggest retailing event of the year – the Back to School Season.  And as students sharpen their pencils, parents are shaving their budgets. A new study out from Deloitte shows that spending will decline this year as tariffs start to weigh on prices. Marc Rosen, CEO of Catalyst, who owns brands like Brooks Brothers and Aeropostle, joins Open Interest to discuss how his company is catering to value-seeking customers. (Corrected headline to better fit content)
--------

items.title: London's Hot Summer: Try Beijing for a Cooler Commute
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>London's underground trains have been extremely hot over the last few months, with temperatures inside uncooled tube carriages climbing above the surface temperature.
While climate change is raising temperatures everywhere, cities like Beijing or New York offer more air conditioned transportation systems capable of keeping their commuters cool. Passengers on London’s busy Central line, for one, have endured heat wave conditions underground every day this summer, according to data recorded by Bloomberg. Joe Wertz reports on what the UK Met Office calls “almost certainly” the warmest summer on record for the country.
--------

items.title: Spar's Retail Pivot: Pulling Back From Europe to Focus on Africa
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>South African retail giant Spar Group is making a major strategic shift. After years of investing in overseas markets, the company is now pulling back from international expansion to focus on high-margin sectors at home like DIY, drugs, liquor and wine. In this report, Spar Group CEO Angelo Swartz sits down with Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja to discuss the firm's bold new vision and what this means for its future growth strategy.
--------

items.title: Japan’s forgotten mascot Monchhichi makes a comeback
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Step aside, Labubu. Japan’s forgotten mascot, Monchhichi, may be making a comeback thanks to Gen Z.

The monkey-like doll’s surge in popularity is not just nostalgic — it marks a fresh opportunity in the booming world of designer toys.</article>

items.title: Cook Hearing Wraps Without Ruling From Judge | Balance of Power 8/29/2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, David Super, Georgetown Law Professor, shares his thoughts on Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook’s hearing and how he thinks the US Supreme Court will rule on this case. Jared Bernstein, former Chair of the White House Council of Economic Advisers, discusses the US ending the De Minimis exemption for shipments and the emergency hearing today on Fed. Governor Lisa Cook ending with no decision. Ukraine's Economy Minister Oleksii Sobolev talks about the EU's plan to use frozen Russian assets to rebuild Ukraine at the end of the war.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Late Edition of Balance Power Begins
00:01:31 - Tyler Kendall on What to Know From Fed. Governor Lisa Cook’s Hearing
00:03:31 - Michael McKee on the Impact of Lisa Cook’s Hearing, Court Hearing Ends Without a Ruling on Fed’s Cook
00:06:35 - David Super, Georgetown Law Professor, on the Legal Implications of Lisa Cook’s Hearing, CDC Firing, How Will SCOTUS View Cook Case
00:13:05 - Stuart Paul on US Consumer Sentiment Remains Resilient Despite Rising Prices
00:15:55 - Jared Bernstein, Fmr. Chair of White House Council of Economic Advisers, Talks Insights on US Consumer Sentiment, Lisa Cook Hearing
00:24:22 - Power Brief: Top Stories Watching Tonight
00:26:02 - Breaking News: US Appeals Court: Trump Global Tariffs Illegal
00:26:27 - Political Panel React to Court Ruling Trump Global Tariffs Illegal, US Consumer Sentiment
00:36:40 - Oleskii Sobolev, Ukraine Minister of Economy, on US Ukraine Teams Meeting on Next Steps, UN Security Council Meeting
--------

items.title: Taylor Swift Wedding Predictions Are the Hottest New Trade
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Gamblers on Polymarket and Kalshi are trying to cash in on the probability of everything from when will Taylor Swift marry Travis Kelce to presidential elections. Sarah Holder, @franmagliones and @antoniabmassa unpack what you need to know on today’s Big Take podcast.

 Listen and subscribe here: https://trib.al/QPQwd5Y

The Big Take from Bloomberg News brings you inside what’s shaping the world's economies with the smartest and most informed business reporters around the world. The context you need on the stories that can move markets. Every afternoon.</article>

items.title: Is The Federal Reserve's Independence at Risk?
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Jared Bernstein, former Chair of the White House Council of Economic Advisers, discusses the US ending the De Minimis exemption for shipments and how this may impact US consumer prices. He also talks about the emergency hearing today on Fed. Governor Lisa Cook ending with no decision, and what President Trump's attempt to fire Cook means for Fed independence, inflation, and public trust. Jared Bernstein talks with Joe Mathieu on the late edition of Bloomberg's "Balance of Power."
--------

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:

He speaks with Joe Mathieu on the late edition of Bloomberg’s “Balance of Power.”

items.title: Trump’s global tariffs found illegal by US appeals court #trump #tariffs #shorts #politics #markets
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Most of President Trump’s global tariffs were ruled illegal by a federal appeals court that found he exceeded his authority in imposing them, but the judges let the levies stay in place while sending the case back to a lower court for further proceedings https://bloom.bg/4lThJ9l</article>

items.title: US Appeals Court Rules Trump's Global Tariffs Illegal
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Most of President Donald Trump’s global tariffs were ruled illegal by a federal appeals court that found he exceeded his authority in imposing them. Stonecourt Capital Partner Rick Davis and Iona University Political Science Professor Jeanne Sheehan Zaino react on "Balance of Power."

Sign up for the Washington Edition newsletter to find out how the worlds of money and politics intersect in the US capital: https://bloom.bg/3KkEimO
